prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite test rework
Primary request: rework this approved Children of Prophecy Mirage Seer so the faction feels visually distinct from The Order and reads more like enlightened desert monks or ascetic mystics
Subject: preserve the exact Mirage Seer identity and silhouette from the input image, including the wrapped face, layered head-wrap, calm raised casting hand, hanging lantern-like ritual focus, lean full-body stance, and transparent background
Style/medium: preserve the same painterly pixel-art-inspired tactics sprite finish, moderate pixelation level, readable broad shapes, and runtime-game sprite clarity as the input image
Composition/framing: preserve the same full-body crop, stance, orientation, and tight sprite bounds
Lighting/mood: solemn, sacred, desert-dusk atmosphere with restrained contrast and a subtle enlightened aura rather than military severity
Color palette: shift away from Order-adjacent rust red and bone-white dominance toward sun-faded saffron yellow, muted moss green, temple gold, pale sand cloth, and a small touch of jade or teal glow in the lantern; the unit should feel warm, spiritual, and distinct from crusader red-and-white troops
Materials/textures: wrapped cloth, pilgrim fabric, lightly worn leather, humble ritual fittings
Constraints: change palette and cloth identity only as needed to sell a distinct Children of Prophecy faction look; preserve the Mirage Seer silhouette, role read, lantern focus, anatomy, and sprite finish; keep it monk-like, enlightened, ascetic, and non-military; exactly one subject; no floor; no scenery; no text; no extra subjects; alpha only outside the outer silhouette; avoid oversized transparent padding
Avoid: Order colors, dark wine red, crusader white-red contrast, plated knight cues, sci-fi cues, glossy rendering, anime rendering, noisy micro-detail, soft blur, cinematic background
